title: hieracium briziflorum subsp. aemuliflorum
intro: Hieracium briziflorum subsp. aemuliflorum, also known as aemuliflorum hawk's beard or Hieracium aemuliflorum, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the dandelion family, native to the British Isles, Europe, and parts of Asia. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including its ability to stimulate digestion and relieve gastrointestinal issues. The health benefits of Hieracium briziflorum subsp. aemuliflorum include its potential to reduce inflammation, alleviate headaches, and support the immune system. In herbal preparations, the plant's leaves and flowers are typically dried and used as a tea or tincture to harness its medicinal properties.
